metrics.narrative: No quantified business case, ROI, or operational pain metric has been documented across Salesforce fields or the single transcribed buyer call (CLM demo 2025-11-05). The rep's Next Step references 'Supplier Onboarding' collaboration and sharing Hackett benchmark content, implying procurement efficiency or spend visibility as a theme, but the buyer has never articulated cycle time reduction, cost savings target, headcount impact, or error-rate improvement on record. champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. Czarina Whitmore (VP Procurement Sourcing & Operations) is the most engaged contact based on the Next Step (2026-07-02), which logs rep outreach to her along with George and Helen, but there is no evidence that Czarina is advocating for Zycus, providing internal intelligence, or driving the deal forward. She may have attended the CLM demo (2025-11-05) but has not replied to subsequent outreach (Pinnacle invite, Hackett content) per the Next Step. Alexander Guzman (Director Strategic Sourcing) and Helen Lee (COE) are also contacts, but their engagement level and advocacy are unknown. A champion must have access to the Economic Buyer (George Jannino, CPO), actively advocate for Zycus over competitors, share internal decision-making intel, and coach the rep on how to win. None of the contacts have demonstrated these behaviors on record. The gap is critical: without a champion in a 10-vendor competitive field, Zycus has no internal advocate to navigate politics, counter competitor narratives, or push the deal through approval gates. The move is to re-engage Czarina or Alexander, deliver value (a targeted insight, a peer reference, a benchmarking session), and earn their advocacy by aligning Zycus to their success and giving them a reason to champion us internally.

competition.narrative: Raymond James is evaluating a crowded field of 10 procurement and contract management vendors: Coupa, SAP Ariba, iValua, JAGGAER, Oracle (the multi-module P2P/procurement suites), and iCertis, Cobblestone, Contract Pod AI, Ironclad, Conga (the CLM specialists). This competitive slate is documented in Salesforce Competitors__c and has not been refined to a shortlist or finalist set despite the deal being 7+ months old. The breadth of the field suggests Raymond James may be running a formal RFP or broad market scan, and the presence of both full-suite players (Coupa, Ariba) and point-solution CLM vendors signals they are evaluating best-of-breed vs integrated platform trade-offs. Coupa and SAP Ariba are the most likely primary threats given their enterprise procurement footprint and financial services customer base. No competitive differentiation, head-to-head comparison, or 'why Zycus' narrative has been documented in Salesforce fields (Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c both null) or discussed on the only buyer call (CLM demo 2025-11-05). The wedge is Merlin agentic AI: Zycus offers Merlin Intake (agentic orchestration), Merlin negotiation agents, and embedded AI across iSupplier/iRisk, which none of the CLM point solutions deliver at scale and which Coupa/Ariba are only beginning to integrate. The activation is to position Merlin as the Phase 2 procurement transformation centerpiece, run a live Merlin demo for George and Czarina, and contrast Zycus AI-native architecture against Coupa/Ariba bolt-on AI. Without this positioning and a competitive review, Zycus risks being seen as another vendor in a crowded field rather than the AI differentiated choice.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, legal review process, MSA/SOW requirements, procurement channel (direct vs through a partner/SI), or InfoSec approval workflow have been discussed or documented. Eduardo Gomez (IT Security Analyst) is listed as a contact role, signaling that InfoSec will review the deal, but there is no record of a security questionnaire, data privacy review, or IT approval gate being initiated. The deal is still in early Qualified stage with no buyer engagement for 8 months, so paper process discovery has not yet been relevant, but it is a gap that will become blocking once the deal reaches commercials and contracting. For a financial services firm (Raymond James) the InfoSec and legal review are likely rigorous and multi-stage, and the procurement channel may involve a preferred SI or advisory firm. The move is to surface the paper process during re-engagement: ask Czarina or George whether Raymond James has standard MSA terms, what the InfoSec review process looks like, and whether a partner or SI will be involved in scoping and implementation.

The Next Step (2026-07-02) logs that the rep invited George to Pinnacle (Zycus customer event) and shared Hackett content with him, but there is no documented reply. On the only buyer call on record (CLM demo 2025-11-05) George was not present; the demo was likely run with Czarina Whitmore (VP Procurement Sourcing & Operations) or her team. Czarina may have budget influence, but for a $400k enterprise procurement suite the CPO is the commercial authority. The gap is critical: without CPO sponsorship the deal cannot move to commercials, pricing, or EB approval. The evidence shows the EB exists organizationally but is unengaged, which is a blocking risk. The move is an executive connect: the deal owner's manager should sponsor a direct CPO-to-CPO introduction to George Jannino to establish sponsorship and confirm budget authority.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer has not shared a formal decision process, timeline, or approval chain. The deal has been in Qualified stage for at least 7 months with no progression to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted, and no second call or buyer-led milestone has occurred since the CLM demo on 2025-11-05. The rep's Next Step (2026-07-02) references tracking 'timing for I&O' (likely Intake & Orchestration or a product launch) and a longer-term collaboration on Supplier Onboarding, but there is no buyer-confirmed timeline, no RFP deadline, no shortlist date, and no stated approval gates. The OpportunityContactRole roster includes George Jannino (CPO), Czarina Whitmore (VP Procurement), Alexander Guzman (Director Strategic Sourcing), Nenad Gmizic (Senior Category Manager), Helen Lee (COE), and Eduardo Gomez (IT Security Analyst), suggesting a cross-functional buying committee (procurement leadership, sourcing ops, IT security), but the sequence of who evaluates when, who shortlists, and who approves budget is unknown. The close date of 2027-01-15 (195 days out) is a rep-entered target, not a buyer-confirmed deadline. The gap is that the buyer has not articulated their process, and 8 months of silence suggests they may be running a slow, multi-vendor evaluation or the initiative has deprioritized. The move is to re-engage George or Czarina and ask directly: what is your evaluation timeline, who is on the decision committee, and what are the next milestones (shortlist, finalist demos, commercials).

gaps: CLM demo call 2025-11-05 is referenced in Salesforce activities but transcript or detailed notes are not available in Avoma (call may predate Avoma integration or was not recorded). Limited ability to extract verbatim buyer quotes, pain statements, or competitive mentions from that call.

No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity via opp_id or account_id + attendee match (only Salesforce activity references exist). Buyer engagement after 2025-11-05 is inferred from rep-side Next Step outreach, not from two-way call content.

AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are null in Salesforce, so AI appetite is inferred from product scope (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI) and rep Next Step, not from a scored AI fit assessment.

MEDDPICC canonical fields (Decision_Maker_Identified__c, Economic_Buyer_Identified__c, Champion_Identified__c, Metrics_Identified__c) are not present in this Salesforce org or were not returned in the SOQL queries. MEDDPICC status is synthesized from OpportunityContactRole, product scope, Next Step, and the single CLM demo reference, not from dedicated MEDDPICC tracking fields.

Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c are null or not present, so time-in-stage and stage velocity are computed from CreatedDate and LastModifiedDate rather than precise stage-entry timestamps.
